compiled_state.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket purge-bridge dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket purge-bridge` subcommand before the implementation exists. This is a safety-critical destructive subcommand — the test MUST verify safety guards fire correctly.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-purge-bridge.sh (new)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-purge-bridge.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket purge-bridge` through the dispatcher (not purge-non-project-tickets.sh directly)
- [ ] Test asserts: when tracker directory does NOT exist, purge-bridge exits non-zero with error message (mount check fires)
- [ ] Test asserts: when tracker directory is NOT writable, purge-bridge exits non-zero with error message (writable check fires)
- [ ] Test asserts: `--dry-run` mode lists individual ticket IDs that would be deleted (not just count) to stdout/stderr
- [ ] Test asserts: deleted ticket IDs are logged to stderr as audit record when actually deleting (not just count)
- [ ] Test asserts: non-matching jira-* tickets are deleted; matching jira-* tickets are preserved; non-jira-* tickets are never touched
- [ ] Test uses a synthetic temp tracker directory with fixture jira-* ticket directories containing CREATE events
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add test file to both dispatcher and lib entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-purge-bridge.sh [test_purge_bridge_safety_checks_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
The test should create a temp directory with a few fake jira-* ticket directories containing minimal CREATE event JSON files. Use TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR env var override (already supported by purge-non-project-tickets.sh) to point at the temp directory. The writable check test can use `chmod 555` on the temp dir, then restore with `chmod 755` in cleanup trap. Do NOT test against a live tickets repo.
